question.text: Sequential settlement of trades on the same day means:

answer.text: Pay-in and pay-out of the first settlement must be completed before commencement of the subsequent settlement
Settlements are done in reverse chronological order
Only one settlement per day is permitted

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CCs clear and settle trades on a sequential basis - first settlement must be completed before the subsequent one begins. **The Trap:** This is relevant when multiple settlement cycles (T+0 and T+1) or holiday backlogs occur on the same day. **Cross-Exam Context:** Sequential settlement ensures orderly processing when multiple settlements coincide. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The pay-out from the first settlement can be used for pay-in of the subsequent settlement.
